items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: Stephen Curry, Al Horford Lead Warriors To 126-121 Comeback Win Over Clippers
items.blurb: Stephen Curry, Al Horford Lead Warriors To 126-121 Comeback Win Over Clippers — Stephen Curry scored 27 of his 35 points in the second half, Al Horford hit four 3-pointers during Golden State's electrifying fourth-quarter comeback, and the Warriors advanced in the NBA's play-in tournament with a 126-121 victory over the Los Angeles Clippers on Wednesday night. Curry's seventh 3-pointer broke a tie

items.headline: Curry drains filthy step-back three to put Warriors ahead in final minute
items.blurb: Curry drains filthy step-back three to put Warriors ahead in final minute — Watch as Stephen Curry shakes off a double team with a filthy step-back move and drains the clutch three-pointer to put the Warriors ahead of the Clippers in the final minute of their play-in game.

items.headline: Warriors’ Green comes up clutch with steals on back-to-back possessions
items.blurb: Warriors’ Green comes up clutch with steals on back-to-back possessions — Watch as Draymond Green comes up clutch for the Warriors with incredible effort plays to force steals on back-to-back possessions in their play-in game against the Clippers.

items.headline: Warriors keep season alive with play-in victory over Clippers
items.blurb: Warriors keep season alive with play-in victory over Clippers — Stephen Curry scored 27 of his 35 points in the second half, Al Horford hit four three-pointers during Golden State’s electrifying fourth-quarter comeback, and the Warriors advanced in the NBA’s play-in tournament with a 126-121 victory over the Los Angeles Clippers on Wednesday night.

items.headline: Warriors elder statesmen's playoff experience helps stun Clippers in comeback win
items.blurb: Warriors elder statesmen's playoff experience helps stun Clippers in comeback win — The Golden State Warriors had to rely on 36-year-old Draymond Green, 39-year-old Al Horford and a hobbled 38-year-old Steph Curry. Down the stretch, the trio showed their playoff pedigree to shock the Los Angeles Clippers.

items.headline: Skip Bayless makes a very controversial claim about Joel Embiid's health
items.blurb: Skip Bayless makes a very controversial claim about Joel Embiid's health — Skip Bayless is sparking controversy this week with his assertion about Joel Embiid.

items.blurb: The 'Multiple 200 three-pointer seasons' quiz — In NBA history, 38 players have made 200 or more three-point field goals in more than one season. How many can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: Clippers’ Leonard gets around Warriors’ Podziemski for two-handed slam
items.blurb: Clippers’ Leonard gets around Warriors’ Podziemski for two-handed slam — Watch as Kawhi Leonard pushes up the court and gets around Brandin Podziemski for the easy two-handed slam against the Warriors.

items.headline: 76ers rookie VJ Edgecombe shows no fear in postseason debut
items.blurb: 76ers rookie VJ Edgecombe shows no fear in postseason debut — Twenty-year-old VJ Edgecombe played his first NBA postseason game Wednesday night for the Philadelphia 76ers. He looked like an experienced veteran in the Sixers' 109-97 win over the Orlando Magic.

items.headline: Magic's disappointing season takes another hit in play-in loss to 76ers
items.blurb: Magic's disappointing season takes another hit in play-in loss to 76ers — The Orlando Magic were expected to be a threat in the Eastern Conference this season after back-to-back playoff appearances and with a talented young roster.

items.headline: NBA makes discipline decision on Hornets' LaMelo Ball for dirty play on Bam Adebayo
items.blurb: NBA makes discipline decision on Hornets' LaMelo Ball for dirty play on Bam Adebayo — Ball is one of the Hornets' most important players, averaging 20.1 points, 7.1 assists, 4.8 rebounds and 1.2 steals per game this season.

items.headline: LaMelo Ball apologizes for dirty foul on Bam Adebayo
items.blurb: LaMelo Ball apologizes for dirty foul on Bam Adebayo — Ball is facing criticism for a play that injured Bam Adebayo.

items.blurb: RealGM Radio With Dubin, Dakhil: Major Questions Of Each Playoff Series — Jared Dubin and Mo Dakhil discuss the spectacular Hornets-Heat and Blazers-Suns Play-In games, run through the rest of their end-of-season awards picks, and talk about the major questions that will define each playoff series. items.headline: LaMelo Ball Fined $35,000, Assessed Flagrant Foul 2 On Bam Adebayo Play
items.blurb: LaMelo Ball Fined $35,000, Assessed Flagrant Foul 2 On Bam Adebayo Play — LaMelo Ball has been fined $35,000 and assessed a Flagrant Foul 2 upon league office review for making unnecessary and reckless contact with Miami Heat center-forward Bam Adebayo that created a significant injury risk. The incident occurred with 11:13 remaining in the second quarter of the Charlotte Hornets' 127-126 overtime

items.headline: 76ers' Andre Drummond turns back the clock in play-in win vs. Magic
items.blurb: 76ers' Andre Drummond turns back the clock in play-in win vs. Magic — Playing without All-Star center Joel Embiid, the Philadelphia 76ers leaned on an All-Star center from the previous decade.

items.headline: Hornets’ LaMelo Ball handed flagrant foul, $60K in fines for actions against Heat
items.blurb: Hornets’ LaMelo Ball handed flagrant foul, $60K in fines for actions against Heat

items.headline: Marcus Smart vows Lakers won’t get punked by Rockets in playoffs
items.blurb: Marcus Smart vows Lakers won’t get punked by Rockets in playoffs — The Los Angeles Lakers are heading into the NBA playoffs on fumes, and they know it.

items.headline: Heat lose to Hornets without Bam Adebayo after potentially dirty LaMelo Ball play
items.blurb: Heat lose to Hornets without Bam Adebayo after potentially dirty LaMelo Ball play — The Miami Heat already had their hands full with the Charlotte Hornets in the 9-10 play-in game. Their task got tougher when they lost their best player to a highly questionable play.

items.headline: NBA Investigating LaMelo Ball For Tripping Bam Adebayo In Play-In
items.blurb: NBA Investigating LaMelo Ball For Tripping Bam Adebayo In Play-In — LaMelo Ball is under investigation by the NBA for tripping Miami Heat center Bam Adebayo during Tuesday's play-in game, which the Charlotte Hornets won 127-126 in overtime. The league has contacted both franchises and will interview Ball, sources said. The incident is under review for a possible flagrant foul 1 or flagrant foul 2

items.headline: Prime Video's first postseason telecast has embarrassing outage
items.blurb: Prime Video's first postseason telecast has embarrassing outage — During a Hornets timeout with 45 seconds left, the broadcast — Prime's first postseason NBA game — went out, replaced by a card that read "Technical Difficulties."

items.headline: Suns' play-in loss highlights worrying Devin Booker trend
items.blurb: Suns' play-in loss highlights worrying Devin Booker trend — The Phoenix Suns dropped their play-in game to the Portland Trail Blazers Tuesday night in part because Portland made almost twice as many three-point shots. The 114-110 loss forced the Suns into an elimination game on Friday and exposed an issue with the team's biggest star.

items.headline: NBA Regular Season Viewership Hits 24-Year High At 170M U.S. Viewers
items.blurb: NBA Regular Season Viewership Hits 24-Year High At 170M U.S. Viewers — The NBA drew 170 million U.S. viewers during the 2025-26 regular season across its four primary broadcast platforms, which is its strongest viewership figures in 24 years and an 86 percent increase over the prior season. The numbers mark Year 1 of the league's 11-year, $76 billion-plus media rights agreement signed in 2024, whi

items.headline: Sean Sweeney Considered NBA's Leading First-Time Head Coach Candidate
items.blurb: Sean Sweeney Considered NBA's Leading First-Time Head Coach Candidate — Sean Sweeney is expected to be one of the leading head coaching candidates during the upcoming offseason cycle. Sweeney joined the San Antonio Spurs as associate head coach under Mitch Johnson last year after four seasons as the lead assistant for Jason Kidd with the Dallas Mavericks. items.headline: ‘I’m one of the best defenders’: Barnes confident heading into post-season
items.blurb: ‘I’m one of the best defenders’: Barnes confident heading into post-season — Toronto Raptors forward Scottie Barnes meets with the media and discusses his defensive capabilities and how he views himself on the defensive end going into the team’s first-round matchup against the Cleveland Cavaliers.

items.headline: ‘I know how much our whole team cares’: Rajakovic on Raptors’ readiness
items.blurb: ‘I know how much our whole team cares’: Rajakovic on Raptors’ readiness — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic meets with the media and discusses the mindset of his group heading into their first-round series against the Cleveland Cavaliers and the focus and care that his team has heading into the series.

items.headline: A'ja Wilson, Aces Sign WNBA Record Three-Year, $5M Supermax Contract
items.blurb: A'ja Wilson, Aces Sign WNBA Record Three-Year, $5M Supermax Contract — A'ja Wilson has agreed to sign a three-year, $5 million supermax contract with the Las Vegas Aces. The deal is the largest in WNBA history and was negotiated by Jade-Li English of Klutch Sports Group. Wilson is a three-time WNBA champion and four-time WNBA Most Valuable Player. Wilson was the first overall pick in the 2018 WNBA

items.headline: LaMelo Ball's game-winner further enhances his star power
items.blurb: LaMelo Ball's game-winner further enhances his star power — No matter what happens from this point forward, LaMelo Ball's clutch game-winning shot on Tuesday night combined with his play all season has sent his star status into a new galaxy.

items.headline: What to expect from Wednesday’s Leonard-Curry clash in Play-In Tournament
items.blurb: What to expect from Wednesday’s Leonard-Curry clash in Play-In Tournament — The Athletic’s Law Murray joins The Raptors Show and gives his thoughts on the Play-In Tournament matchup between the Los Angeles Clippers and Golden State Warriors that will see two all-time greats in Kawhi Leonard and Stephen Curry go head-to-head.

items.blurb: Deni Avdija cements star status with phenomenal play-in performance — Avdija announced himself in a big way during the Blazers' play-in win over the Suns.

items.blurb: Dissecting which Raptors have most to gain in opening round series — The Raptors Show’s Blake Murphy and Matt Bonner delve into the Toronto Raptors’ lineup going into their first-round series against the Cleveland Cavaliers and which players on the team could benefit from strong series performances.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's 41 Points Leads Blazers Back To Playoffs For First Time Since 2021
items.blurb: Deni Avdija's 41 Points Leads Blazers Back To Playoffs For First Time Since 2021 — Deni Avdija scored 41 points to go with 12 assists and seven rebounds in the Portland Trail Blazers' 114-110 win at the Phoenix Suns to clinch the No. 7 seed in the Western Conference Playoffs. The Blazers hadn't reached the playoffs since 2021 when they were eliminated in the first round by the Denver Nuggets in si

items.headline: One factor that could impact Heat trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo
items.blurb: One factor that could impact Heat trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo — The Heat are expected to pursue Antetokounmpo, but one factor may determine their ability to land the star.

items.headline: Stephen Curry: Erupts for 35 points in victory
items.blurb: Stephen Curry: Erupts for 35 points in victory — Curry supplied 35 points (12-23 FG, 7-12 3Pt, 4-5 FT), four assists, one rebound and one steal across 36 minutes in Wednesday's 126-121 win over the Clippers in the Play-In Tournament. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Russell Westbrook Will Return To Kings If Wanted Back
items.blurb: Russell Westbrook Will Return To Kings If Wanted Back — Russell Westbrook's season with the Sacramento Kings ended with the team tied for last place in the Western Conference and Westbrook sidelined due to injury. Despite the downer ending to the year, Westbrook would like to return to Sacramento next season. "If I'm welcomed back, then I'll be back," Westbrook said when asked if he'd return to th

items.blurb: 2026 NBA Play-In Snapshots (April 14): Hornets/Heat, Blazers/Suns — What happened in two snapshots in the NBA Play-In on Tuesday, April 14, 2026.

items.headline: Bulls are the less-heralded winners of first play-in night
items.blurb: Bulls are the less-heralded winners of first play-in night — The Bulls weren't in the NBA's play-in tournament for the first time in four seasons. That doesn't mean they didn't have something to root for.

items.headline: Derrick Jones: Starts second half Wednesday
items.blurb: Derrick Jones: Starts second half Wednesday — Jones (ankle) started the second half of Wednesday's Play-In Tournament game against the Warriors, per the broadcast. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Derrick Jones: Leaves for locker room
items.blurb: Derrick Jones: Leaves for locker room — Jones exited to the locker room in the second quarter of Wednesday's Play-In Tournament game against the Warriors due to an apparent right ankle injury, Tomer Azarly of ClutchPoints.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Stephen Curry: Returns Wednesday
items.blurb: Stephen Curry: Returns Wednesday — Curry (undisclosed) returned to Wednesday's Play-In Tournament game against the Clippers with 7:59 remaining in the second quarter, per the broadcast. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Stephen Curry: Exits to locker room
items.blurb: Stephen Curry: Exits to locker room — Curry (undisclosed) exited to the locker room during the first quarter of Wednesday's Play-In Tournament game against the Clippers, Anthony Slater of ESPN.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
